notes: non-discriminating on this eval: prompt was explicit enough that baseline matched content quality
Strong discrimination across all 3 evals: with_skill 15/15 vs without_skill 5/15.
Baseline's biggest structural gaps: no .claude/handoffs/ location, no YAML frontmatter, no matching headings, no .gitignore management.

Eval 3 (near-complete-chronicle-candidate) is the weakest discriminator of the three (4/4 vs 3/4) — a sufficiently detailed prompt let the baseline produce comparable CONTENT quality; the skill's remaining edge there is structural consistency (fixed headings, machine-parseable frontmatter) rather than content quality.

Frontmatter status enum (in_progress|complete) may be too coarse — eval 3's with_skill run needed body text to convey 'needs review', while baseline's freeform format expressed it more directly. Consider adding a needs_review status value in the next iteration.
